document_name: National Geological Survey Data for Nevada, Siberia, Canadian Northern Territories
description: Geological, seismic, and groundwater datasets for the three candidate sites, used to evaluate site suitability and risk for underground construction.
recency_requirement: Most recent available year (within the last 3 years)
steps_to_find: Contact USGS for Nevada data via the USGS Earth Explorer portal.
Request Russian geological data from the Russian Geological Survey (RGS) through their online portal or formal request.
Download Canadian geological data from Natural Resources Canada (NRCan) via GeoGratis or the Canadian Geoscience Data Centre.
Verify metadata and data quality for each dataset.
access_difficulty: Medium – public datasets but may require registration or formal data‑request procedures.

document_name: Environmental Impact Assessment Guidelines and Regulations (EPA, Russian Ministry, Canada)
description: Official guidelines and regulatory requirements for conducting environmental impact assessments for large infrastructure projects in the United States, Russia, and Canada.
steps_to_find: Search the EPA website for the latest EIA guidance documents.
Navigate the Russian Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment portal for current EIA regulations.
Access Environment Canada’s EIA policy documents via the Environment and Climate Change Canada website.
Download and compile the most recent versions.
access_difficulty: Easy – publicly available on government websites.

document_name: Existing Social Control and Information Regulation Laws (US, Russia, Canada)
description: Statutes, regulations, and policy documents governing surveillance, social control, and information access in the United States, Russia, and Canada.
steps_to_find: Search legal databases (Westlaw, LexisNexis) for relevant federal and state statutes.
Access Russian legal portals (e.g., Consultant.ru) for national laws on surveillance and information control.
Retrieve Canadian federal and provincial legislation on privacy and information access from the Justice Laws Website.
Extract key provisions and annotate for relevance.
access_difficulty: Medium – requires access to legal databases or formal requests to government portals.

document_name: Existing Resource Management and Circular Economy Policies (US, Russia, Canada)
description: National policies and regulatory frameworks on water, energy, waste management, and circular economy initiatives that will inform the Resource Management Philosophy.
steps_to_find: Search the U.S. EPA and Department of Energy websites for water and energy policies.
Retrieve Russian federal environmental policy documents from the Ministry of Natural Resources.
Download Canadian circular economy and waste management policies from Environment Canada.
Compile policy summaries.

document_name: Existing Mental Health Research on Long‑Term Confinement (NASA, Submarine, Space)
description: Peer‑reviewed studies and reports on the psychological effects of confinement in isolated environments such as space missions, submarine crews, and long‑term research stations.
steps_to_find: Search PubMed and PsycINFO for studies on confinement and isolation.
Access NASA Technical Reports Server for relevant psychological research.
Retrieve reports from the U.S. Navy’s Submarine Psychological Research Program.
Download PDFs and summarize key findings.
access_difficulty: Medium – some paywalls and institutional access required.

document_name: Existing Cybersecurity Threat Landscape for Critical Infrastructure (NIST, CISA)
description: Official reports and threat intelligence on cyber threats to critical infrastructure, providing baseline data for the Security Vulnerability Assessment Plan.
steps_to_find: Visit the NIST website and download the latest Cybersecurity Framework updates.
Access CISA’s Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Threat Landscape reports.
Compile threat vectors, vulnerability statistics, and mitigation recommendations.
Summarize findings for the assessment plan.
access_difficulty: Easy – publicly available on NIST and CISA websites.
